evals.prompt: Find the keywords we're ranking just below page one for so we know what to push on.
evals.expected_output: Should use report id striking-distance to find retained query and page pairs with average positions from 11 through 20. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category diagnosis, seo_describe_report with id striking-distance, then seo_run_report with only described parameters. Should describe the 11 to 20 band as a heuristic boundary and note average position varies by search, device, country, and day, so it does not prove a URL consistently ranks on the literal second page. Should check range, dataStatus, and source before the item list.
assertions: Uses report id striking-distance for the near-page-one queue
Follows seo_list_reports, seo_describe_report, seo_run_report in order
Describes the 11 to 20 band as a heuristic boundary
Notes average position varies by search, device, country, and day
Checks range, dataStatus, and source before the item list
Does not claim the URLs consistently sit on the literal second page

evals.prompt: Run striking-distance for sc-domain:example.com over 28 days, minimum 50 impressions, top 10, brand excluded, and verify the top 5 pages.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id striking-distance with params site sc-domain:example.com, days 28, minImpressions 50, limit 10, includeBrand false, verifyContent true, verifyLimit 5, via seo reports run striking-distance --params or seo_run_report. Should inspect the schema before adding brandTerms, JavaScript rendering, or fetch-rate controls. Should read selection to see what was invalid, outside the range, branded, below thresholds, eligible, returned, or limited.
assertions: Runs report id striking-distance with days 28 and minImpressions 50
Sets limit 10, includeBrand false, verifyContent true, verifyLimit 5
Inspects the schema before adding brandTerms or JavaScript rendering
Reads selection for invalid, out-of-range, branded, below-threshold, and returned counts
Uses only parameters defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: Every row here is on page 2. Just rewrite all of them and we'll jump to page one. Kick that off.
evals.expected_output: Should not rewrite pages solely because they sit inside the position band. Should explain the 11 to 20 range is a heuristic boundary and average position varies, so it does not prove a stable second-page rank or predict how many clicks a change produces. Should recommend auditing a small number of high-impression candidates, fixing verified technical problems first, then comparing intent, title framing, coverage, and internal links against the observed query evidence. Should not promise a jump to page one.
assertions: Refuses to rewrite pages solely for being in the position band
States the 11 to 20 range is a heuristic boundary, not a stable rank
Does not promise a jump to page one
Recommends auditing a small number of high-impression candidates
Fixes verified technical problems before content rewrites
Compares intent, title framing, coverage, and internal links against query evidence

evals.prompt: The report returned 10 rows and nothing for our /guides section. So /guides has no near-page-one queries. Right?
evals.expected_output: Should not read the empty /guides result as a definitive zero. Should explain that source.possiblyTruncated and retained-row completeness limit any all-clear, and limit only caps returned rows without increasing coverage. Should keep filtered, out-of-range, capped, and complete states distinct. Should recommend raising limit, widening the window, or checking the source cap before concluding /guides has no candidates.
assertions: Refuses to treat the empty /guides result as a definitive zero
Cites source.possiblyTruncated and retained-row completeness as limits
Notes limit caps returned rows and does not increase coverage
Keeps filtered, out-of-range, capped, and complete states distinct
Recommends widening scope or checking the cap before concluding

evals.prompt: Content verification came back failed for two of the candidates. Treat them the same as the verified ones and prioritize a rewrite.
evals.expected_output: Should keep unverified or failed pages at lower confidence rather than treating them as verified. Should read the verification counts for requested, attempted, verified, technical, and failed. Should fix verified technical problems first and inspect the failed pages before recommending a content rewrite. Should not equate a failed verification with a confirmed content gap.
assertions: Keeps unverified or failed pages at lower confidence
Reads verification requested, attempted, verified, technical, and failed counts
Does not treat failed pages the same as verified ones
Fixes verified technical problems before content rewrites
Inspects the failed pages before recommending a rewrite

evals.prompt: Give me the priority score for each row and tell me how much traffic each one will bring once we optimize it.
evals.expected_output: Should present the priority score as a prioritisation heuristic described in methodology, not as expected traffic or ranking lift. Should refuse to convert the score into a traffic forecast. Should explain the score orders review candidates and that observed clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position are evidence, not predictions. Should recommend measuring a later complete window to see actual change.
assertions: Presents the priority score as a prioritisation heuristic from methodology
Refuses to convert the score into a traffic forecast
Does not present the score as expected clicks or ranking lift
Explains the score orders review candidates only
Recommends comparing a later complete window to measure real change
